title: Introduction to AirSensor2
text: following example creates PurpleAir_synoptic (“pas”) object recent measurements PurpleAir sensors Washington state.
code: # AirSensor2 package library(AirSensor2)  # Set user's PurpleAir_API_READ_KEY source('global_vars.R') setAPIKey("PurpleAir-read", PurpleAir_API_READ_KEY)  # Initialize spatial datasets initializeMazamaSpatialUtils()  # All sensors in Washington state WA_pas <-   pas_createNew(     countryCodes = "US",     stateCodes = "WA",     lookbackDays = 1,     location_type = 0   )    pas_leaflet(WA_pas)

title: Introduction to AirSensor2
text: AirSensor2 package handles variety data sources within world low-cost sensors various functions tailored specifics data source. Functions names form <source>_<action>() designed perform particular <action> data provided specific <source>. Currently supported sources data include: Clarity OpenAQ PurpleAir addition, functions names form <structure>_<action>() designed perform particular <action> data converted particular <structure> (aka “class”). Currently supported PurpleAir data structures include: pas – PurpleAirSynoptic. Data many PurpleAir sensors specific point time. pas object simple tibble. pat – PurupleAirTimeseries. Time series data specific PurpleAir sensor. pat object list two tibbles: meta data. synoptic – Generic synoptic data many sensors specific point time. synoptic object simple tibble. Synoptic time series dataframes returned Clarity_~ OpenAQ_~ functions dedicated functions easily manipulated using dplyr package.

what: Process and Display Data from Air Quality Sensors
title: Process and Display Data from Air Quality Sensors
code: Utilities for working with air quality monitoring data from low-cost sensors with a focus on small particulates (PM2.5). Initial focus is on sensors produced by 'PurpleAir' <https://www2.purpleair.com>.

title: Process and Display Data from Air Quality Sensors
text: AirSensor2 package refactoring core data access manipulation functionality found AirSensor package. goal AirSensor2 create lean, generic package easily maintained focused data ingest basic data manipulation. initially focused data PurpleAir sensors, AirSensor2 aims provide tools can used wide variety low-cost air quality sensors local communities can easily access, QC visualize local air quality. Development original AirSensor package supported South Coast Air Quality Management District funds US EPA STAR grant. Additional support provided US Forest Service AirFire Research Team. AirSensor2 currently seeking sponsors development. Initial development R package supported funding following institutions: USFS AirFire Research Team South Coast Air Quality Management District

what: Create an OpenAQ locations dataset — OpenAQ_createLocations
title: Create an OpenAQ locations dataset — OpenAQ_createLocations
text: Downloads location metadata OpenAQ returns enhanced data frame suitable analysis mapping MazamaLocationUtils.

title: Create an OpenAQ locations dataset — OpenAQ_createLocations
code: OpenAQ_createLocations(   countryCodes = NULL,   stateCodes = NULL,   counties = NULL,   lookbackDays = 1,   providers = NULL,   manufacturers = NULL,   is_monitor = NULL,   limit = 1000,   api_key = NULL )

title: Create an OpenAQ locations dataset — OpenAQ_createLocations
text: countryCodes Optional ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country codes used subset data. stateCodes Optional ISO 3166-2 alpha-2 state codes used subset data. counties Optional U.S. county names 5-digit FIPS codes used subset data. lookbackDays Number days look back locations recent data. Use `lookbackDays = 0` include historical locations. providers Optional character vector OpenAQ provider names export prefixes used subset data. manufacturers Optional character vector manufacturer names used subset data. is_monitor Optional logical used filter results regulatory monitors (`TRUE`) air sensors (`FALSE`). `NULL`, included. limit Maximum number locations request per API call. Values greater 1000 reset 1000. api_key OpenAQ API read key. `NULL`, key obtained `MazamaCoreUtils::getAPIKey("OPENAQ")`.

title: Create an OpenAQ locations dataset — OpenAQ_createLocations
text: data frame enhanced OpenAQ location metadata.

title: Create an OpenAQ locations dataset — OpenAQ_createLocations
text: function can filter locations country, state, county, provider, manufacturer, monitor type. can also limit results locations recent data using `lookbackDays`. Returned data enhanced standardized identifiers spatial metadata timezone, countryCode, stateCode. Valid values `countryCodes`, `providers`, `manufacturers` can explored [OpenAQ_getCountries()], [OpenAQ_getProviders()], [OpenAQ_getManufacturers()].

title: Create an OpenAQ locations dataset — OpenAQ_createLocations
code: # \donttest{ try({   if (interactive()) {     initializeMazamaSpatialUtils()      # NOTE:  Read environment vars from .env file with dotenv::load_dot_env()     OPENAQ_API_KEY <- Sys.getenv("OPENAQ_API_KEY")      locations <-       OpenAQ_createLocations(         countryCodes = "US",         stateCodes = "IL",         counties = "Cook",         api_key = OPENAQ_API_KEY       )      table(locations$provider_name)      clarity <- locations %>% dplyr::filter(provider_name == "Clarity")     airnow <- locations %>% dplyr::filter(provider_name == "AirNow")     airgradient <- locations %>% dplyr::filter(provider_name == "AirGradient")      map <-       MazamaLocationUtils::table_leaflet(         clarity,         extraVars = c("deviceDeploymentID", "start", "end", "owner_name", "provider_name"),         radius = 5, fillColor = "blue"       )      map <- MazamaLocationUtils::table_leafletAdd(       map,       airnow,       extraVars = c("deviceDeploymentID", "start", "end", "owner_name", "provider_name"),       radius = 10, fillColor = "black"     )      map <- MazamaLocationUtils::table_leafletAdd(       map,       airgradient,       extraVars = c("deviceDeploymentID", "start", "end", "owner_name", "provider_name"),       radius = 5, fillColor = "red"     )      print(map)   } }, silent = FALSE) # }

title: Plot the reporting lifespan of OpenAQ locations — OpenAQ_lifespanPlot
text: function useful exploring locations long reporting histories appear briefly OpenAQ archive. can use `dplyr::filter()` `dplyr::arrange()` pre-process `locations` data frame plotting. `showLocation = TRUE`, typical values `locationIdentifier` `"id"` `"locationID"`.

title: Plot the reporting lifespan of OpenAQ locations — OpenAQ_lifespanPlot
code: # \donttest{ try({   if (interactive()) {     initializeMazamaSpatialUtils()      # NOTE:  Read environment vars from .env file with dotenv::load_dot_env()     OPENAQ_API_KEY <- Sys.getenv("OPENAQ_API_KEY")      locations <-       OpenAQ_createLocations(         countryCodes = "US",         stateCodes = "IL",         counties = "Cook",         api_key = OPENAQ_API_KEY       )      # Plot all lifespans     locations %>%       OpenAQ_lifespanPlot()      # Label locations with OpenAQ integer ids     locations %>%       OpenAQ_lifespanPlot(         showLocation = TRUE,         locationIdentifier = "locationName",         cex = 0.6,         lwd = 2,         moreSpace = 0.3       )      # Arrange by lifespan before plotting     locations %>%       dplyr::mutate(lifespan = .data$datetime_last - .data$datetime_first) %>%       dplyr::arrange(.data$lifespan) %>%       OpenAQ_lifespanPlot(         showLocation = TRUE,         locationIdentifier = "locationName",         cex = 0.6,         lwd = 2,         moreSpace = 0.3       )   } }, silent = FALSE) # }

title: Example historical PurpleAir synoptic dataset — example_pas_historical
text: `example_pas_historical` dataset provides quickly loadable version *pas* object practice code examples. object contains fewer metadata fields `example_pas_~` objects intended use [pas_lifespanPlot()]. dataset contains data sensors Washington Oregon generated 2024-03-12 running:
code: library(AirSensor2)  initializeMazamaSpatialUtils()  source("global_vars.R") # contains PurpleAir_API_READ_KEY  example_pas_historical <-   pas_createNew(     api_key = PurpleAir_API_READ_KEY,     fields = PurpleAir_PAS_MINIMAL_FIELDS,     countryCodes = "US",     stateCodes = "WA",     counties = "Okanogan",     lookbackDays = 0,            # all years     location_type = 0            # outdoor only   )  save(example_pas_historical, file = "data/example_pas_historical.rda")

what: Create a new PurpleAir timeseries dataset. — pat_create
title: Create a new PurpleAir timeseries dataset. — pat_create
text: Create `pat` object specific `sensor_index`. function splits requested time range multi-day intervals (required PurpleAir API) makes repeated calls `pat_downloadParseRawData()`. `sleep` parameter waits small amount time API requests. PurpleAir API respond "rate limiting" errors unless sleep set appropriately. `parallel = TRUE`, `sleep` ignored.

title: Create a new PurpleAir timeseries dataset. — pat_create
code: pat_create(   api_key = NULL,   pas = NULL,   sensor_index = NULL,   startdate = NULL,   enddate = NULL,   timezone = "UTC",   average = 0,   fields = PurpleAir_PAT_QC_FIELDS,   read_keys = NULL,   sleep = 0.5,   parallel = FALSE,   baseUrl = "https://api.purpleair.com/v1/sensors",   verbose = TRUE )

title: Create a new PurpleAir timeseries dataset. — pat_create
text: api_key PurpleAir API Read Key. `api_key = NULL`, obtained using `getAPIKey("PurpleAir-read")`. See [MazamaCoreUtils::setAPIKey()]. pas Previously generated *pas* object containing `sensor_index`. sensor_index PurpleAir sensor unique identifier. startdate Desired start time (ISO 8601) `POSIXct`. enddate Desired end time (ISO 8601) `POSIXct`. timezone Olson timezone used interpret dates. average Temporal averaging minutes performed PurpleAir. One : 0 (raw), 10, 30, 60 (hour), 360, 1440 (day). fields Character string PurpleAir field names Get Sensor Data API. read_keys Optional, comma separated list sensor read_keys required private devices. separate api_key sensor read_key. Submit multiple keys separating comma (,) character example: `"key-one,key-two,key-three"`. sleep Seconds sleep API requests. parallel Logical specifying whether attempt simultaneous downloads using [parallel::mcparallel()]. (available Windows.) baseUrl Base URL PurpleAir API. verbose Logical controlling generation warning error messages.

Added AirGradient_applyCorrection().

title: AirSensor2 0.5.1
text: pat_createNew() now checks removes duplicate timesteps guarantees returned object class name ‘sts’ “SingleTimeSeries” can work functions MazamaTimeSeries. Fixed bug pat_toMonitor() always complained missing fields. Fixed bug sensor_index field returned PurpleAir sometimes interpreted integer instead character. Example bug:
code: > MazamaCoreUtils::parseDatetime("2023-05-02 03:59:59", timezone = "UTC") %>% as.numeric() [1] 1.683e+09

title: AirSensor2 0.5.0
text: Version 0.5 introduces changes default parameters requested making data requests PurpleAir API. past, data free, functions download many potentially relevant parameters possible. Now payment system place, AirSensor2 focused downloading parameters required accomplish task. Many low level PurpleAir data access functions thus behave differently. Package dependency updates: Modified pas_downloadParseRawData() defaults query non-measurement fields defined PurplAir_SENSOR_METADATA_FIELDS. Added pas_filterDate() help find historical sensor data. Added pas_filterNearMonitor() associate sensors closest available monitor mts_monitor object AirMonitor package. Renamed pas_get() pas_pull(). Updated pas_createNew() pas_leaflet() default working non-measurement fields defined PurplAir_SENSOR_METADATA_FIELDS. Added pas_lifespanPlot() show PurpleAir sensors producing data. PurpleAir_PAS_MINIMAL_FIELDS PurpleAir_PAS_METADATA_FIELDS PurpleAir_PAS_AVG_PM25_FIELDS PurpleAir_PAList_PM25_FIELDS PurpleAir_PAT_QC_FIELDS PurpleAir_PAT_EPA_HOURLY_FIELDS Added sleep parameter pat_create() avoid “rate limiting” errors PurpleAir API. Removed PurpleAir_createNewMonitor(). Added pat_toMonitor(). Renamed pat_createNew() pat_create(). Added pat_createRaw().

title: AirSensor2 0.2.0
text: Added full support PurpleAir API described https://api.purpleair.com. Improvements include wrapper functions PurpleAir API endpoints: PurpleAir_PurpleAir_checkApiKey() PurpleAir_getSensorData(), PurpleAir_getSensorHistoryCSV(), PurpleAir_getSensorHistory(), PurpleAir_getSensorsData() PurpleAir_createGroup(), PurpleAir_createMember(), PurpleAir_delteGroup(), PurpleAir_deleteMember(), PurpleAir_getGroupDetail(), PurpleAir_getGroupsList(), PurpleAir_getMemberData(), PurpleAir_getMemberHistory(), PurpleAir_getMembemrsData() Additional updates include: Default fields strings PurpleAir_DATA_PM25_FIELDS PurpleAir_PAT_QC_FIELDS Added fields argument pas_downloadParseRawData(). Now using 10-character geohash locationID. (See geohashTools reasons use geohash unique location identifier.) Updated pas_downloadParseRawData() use new wrapper functions. Updated pas_enhanceRawData() pas_createNew() harmonize argument names. Addded pat_downloadParseRawData() using new wrapper functions. Added pat_create() example_pat dataset. Added utility functions: pat_check(), pat_isValid(), pat_isEmpty(), pat_distinct(), pat_getData(), pat_getMeta().
